name: Abu al-Hutuf Ansari
title: Awakened Brother
shortDescription: Sa'd ibn Harith's brother who was with him in the Kufan army, was transformed upon hearing Imam Hussain's (AS) oppressed cry, and attained martyrdom.
fullStory:
Abu al-Hutuf ibn al-Harith al-Ansari was a Kharijite of Kufa and the brother of Sa'd ibn al-Harith. These two brothers were known for their enmity towards the Prophet's (PBUH) family and Amir al-Mu'minin (AS), which is why they joined Umar ibn Sa'd's army.

On Ashura, Abu al-Hutuf, alongside his brother, watched the martyrdom of Imam Hussain's (AS) companions and family one by one. Having come to fight the Imam, on the afternoon of Ashura, he was transformed upon hearing Imam Hussain's (AS) lonely cry: 'Is there any helper to help us?' followed by the weeping and wailing of women and children.

This old man's conscience awakened. He and his brother Sa'd suddenly realized the depth of the tragedy and the falsehood of their path. At that very moment, they separated from the Kufan army, drew their swords, and attacked their former comrades.

Abu al-Hutuf and Sa'd attained martyrdom together in the final moments of Ashura after killing a number of enemy soldiers, achieving eternal salvation.
quoteArabic: أَلا ناصِرٌ يَنْصُرُنا... أَلا ذابٌّ يَذُبُّ عَنْ حَرَمِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ...
quote: (The call that transformed them:) Is there any helper to help us? Is there any defender to defend the sanctuary of the Messenger of God?
